trajectory.thought: I need to use the python_exec tool to interact with the customer service agent system. Let me first explore what's available in the environment to understand how to connect to the agent.

At this step, I would tell the customer service agent: "I'd like to exchange my water bottle for a bigger one." This communicates the product to exchange and the reason (want a bigger size), which is the core of step 2.

For step 3, I would tell the agent: "I'd also like to exchange the desk lamp. I want a brighter one. For the replacement, please prioritize an AC adapter-powered lamp first; if that's not available, a battery-powered one; and USB-powered only as a last resort."

When the agent presents desk lamp replacement options, I will evaluate them by power source in priority order: (1) AC adapter — if any AC-powered brighter desk lamp is available, select it; (2) battery-powered — if no AC adapter option exists; (3) USB-powered — only as a last resort. I will apply this preference rule to whichever replacement choices the agent offers for the desk lamp exchange.

All prior steps were reasoning-only with no actual tool executions performed (no live agent was contacted). The current step is also conditional ("if the agent asks for confirmation") with no tool action needed. The plan's decision rule is clear: if a confirmation prompt is received, confirm only the desk lamp exchange and decline/skip the water bottle exchange.
